figures.caption: Fig 3: Framework established for the models’ evaluation and testing.
Fig 6: Images from DMID dataset. (a-d) original images; (e-h) ground-truth masks; (i-l) predicted masks at a threshold of 0.5, and; (m-p) predicted masks overlaid on the images.
Fig 2: Sample images from (a-b) INbreast and (c-d) DMID datasets. Each column shows the original image, the ground-truth annotation, and the corresponding mask overlaid on the image.
Table 2: List of the lightweight models and their complexity considering an input tensor of 1 × 1024× 1024.

Table 3: Segmentation performance and model complexity comparison. The best model was selected based on the Dice score.
Table 5: Segmentation performance as a function of threshold on the external dataset (DMID). A threshold of 0.5 was used as a reference.
Fig 7: Segmentation performance (Dice, IoU, and Recall) as a function of threshold on the external dataset.
Fig 5: Wilcoxon signed-rank p − values across the five cross-validation folds. In parentheses is the result of Bonferroni correction.
